western.definition: Ability to dictate outcomes through force/regulation; centralized authority over processes; minimizing unpredictability through standardization.

relational.definition: Understanding field dynamics well enough to work with them; influence through alignment with natural patterns; multiple redundant pathways increase actual control over outcomes.

hidden_variables.name: Relationship quality
hidden_variables.description: The quality of cooperation between actors and the system being controlled.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Force-based control treats the controlled system as adversarial; relationship quality is invisible to compliance metrics.

hidden_variables.name: Pattern recognition depth
hidden_variables.description: Practitioner's accumulated familiarity with the system's natural dynamics.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Centralized authority substitutes rules for embodied understanding.

hidden_variables.name: Adaptive capacity
hidden_variables.description: How well the system reorganizes under perturbation.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Standardization and force-based control measure compliance, not the system's ability to absorb change.

translation_protocol.when_to_translate: When stakeholders use 'control' but disagree on whether outcomes should be achieved through force/regulation or through alignment with field dynamics.
translation_protocol.how: Ask: 'Are you looking for more reliable outcomes through redundant systems, or more centralized management of the current approach?'
examples: Person A: 'We need better control over the infrastructure monitoring system.' Clarifying response: 'When you say control, are you looking for more reliable outcomes through redundant systems, or more centralized management of the current approach?'
